commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should review the message-delivery risk and decide whether the supplied proof is enough to merge.; Cleared: The diff touches reply routing and tests only; it does not change dependencies, workflows, credentials, permissions, generated assets, or package supply-chain surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR changes reply routing so `sessions_send` inter-session webchat handoffs can inherit a persisted external delivery context and adds focused routing/dispatch regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +141. Total +177 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection plus the linked Feishu logs give a high-confidence path: seed a session with a Feishu `deliveryContext`, inject a `sessions_send` inter-session webchat turn, and verify current main keeps the reply on webchat instead of the persisted external route.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should review the message-delivery risk and decide whether the supplied proof is enough to merge.

target.title: fix(reply): preserve sessions_send external routes
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88044.

- preserve a target session's persisted external delivery context for internal `sessions_send` handoffs that enter through the internal `webchat` surface
- mark inherited external routes in `resolveEffectiveReplyRoute` so dispatch can route them without making `sessions_send` look like a Feishu/Telegram/etc. user turn
- keep ordinary webchat turns on their live webchat route, and ignore persisted webchat routes for inter-session handoffs

## Behavior

Before this change, an agent-to-agent `sessions_send` turn could arrive as internal `webchat` and then fail to deliver a reply back to the existing Feishu session, even when the target session still had a valid external `deliveryContext`.

After this change, only `sessions_send` inter-session handoffs with a persisted deliverable external `deliveryContext` inherit that route. Normal Control UI/webchat turns continue to use their live webchat route.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_send` replies can route through a target session's persisted external delivery context instead of being trapped as internal `webchat`.

Real environment tested: original issue #88044 reports a real OpenClaw 2026.5.27 Feishu/Lark setup with 7 agents, `tools.agentToAgent.enabled=true`, and `tools.sessions.visibility="all"`. The after-fix proof ran on patched branch `e095bc4a32ef` using that same route shape: target session key `agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx`, persisted `deliveryContext.channel="feishu"`, incoming `InputProvenance.kind="inter_session"`, `sourceTool="sessions_send"`, and live provider/surface/originating channel all `webchat`. To avoid live credentials in the PR, the proof registered a Feishu outbound adapter and exercised the production effective-route resolver plus durable external-channel delivery boundary.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --require ./node_modules/tsx/dist/preflight.cjs --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs .artifacts/proof/sessions-send-feishu-route-proof.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "inputProvenance": {
    "kind": "inter_session",
    "sourceTool": "sessions_send",
    "sourceChannel": "webchat"
  },
  "sessionKey": "agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx",
  "persistedDeliveryContext": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work"
  },
  "liveTurnSurface": {
    "Provider": "webchat",
    "Surface": "webchat",
    "OriginatingChannel": "webchat",
    "OriginatingTo": "session:dashboard"
  },
  "effectiveRoute": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work",
    "inheritedExternalRoute": true
  },
  "durableSendResult": {
    "status": "sent",
    "results": [
      {
        "channel": "feishu",
        "messageId": "om_after_fix_proof_1",
        "chatId": "user:ou_123"
      }
    ]
  },
  "feishuSends": [
    {
      "adapter": "registered feishu outbound.sendText",
      "kind": "text",
      "to": "user:ou_123",
      "accountId": "work",
      "text": "QA-SESSIONS-SEND-FEISHU-PROOF",
      "replyToId": null,
      "threadId": null
    }
  ],
  "observed": "PASS: sessions_send webchat handoff used persisted Feishu deliveryContext"
}
```

Observed result after fix: the same route shape from #88044 now resolves the final reply route to `channel="feishu"`, `to="user:ou_123"`, `accountId="work"`, marks it `inheritedExternalRoute=true`, and the external-channel delivery boundary calls the registered Feishu outbound adapter with the proof marker. The focused regression suite also passed 198 tests and confirms normal webchat turns still stay on `webchat`.

What was not tested: a fresh live Feishu tenant run against this patched branch was not rerun; the live Feishu evidence remains the original issue's redacted runtime logs, paired here with after-fix patched-branch external-channel delivery proof for the same persisted Feishu route shape.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
